$comment: An easter egg, and a proof. John Stuart Mill's On Liberty (1859, Project Gutenberg #34901, public domain) published NATIVELY into the UOR content-addressable substrate a self-verifying linked-data Merkle-DAG whose root did:holo commits to every word. It demonstrates the repository's whole claim on one famous artifact: a content-addressable substrate that unlocks self-verifying, serverless applications. Not listed in the Hub catalog reachable only by its did:holo / over MCP. Built by build-liberty.mjs.
name: On Liberty
summary: On Liberty by John Stuart Mill, published as a verified object — the whole book proves itself genuine from its own content.

resources.name: On Liberty (UOR Merkle-DAG)
resources.description: The whole book as a self-verifying linked-data κ-DAG (Book → Chapters → text). Each node is schema.org JSON-LD with a did:holo; the root commits to every word. Fetch it and re-derive every hash provenance + integrity, no trusted server (Law L5). Provenance: dcterms:source pins the exact Project Gutenberg #34901 bytes by sha-256 κ.

tools.name: read_liberty
tools.description: Return a self-verifying chapter of On Liberty as a UOR object the agent can re-derive (Law L5). Args: chapter (0=Introduction, 1..5 = the chapters) or find (a phrase; returns the chapter containing it). With no args, returns the book root object.

tools.name: verify_object
tools.description: Re-derive a UOR object's did from its content and report whether it self-verifies (Law L5).

description.p: A text you read should be one you can prove is unaltered, with no server to trust.
The complete book is published as a content-addressed object graph; every chapter re-derives from its own bytes in the browser (Law L5).

ul: The full text of On Liberty
A reference holospace for published works
